arts.ttl: Alfie Chang’s next step outlined as Birmingham City midfielder recovers from 14 months out
arts.sum: Blues manager Chris Davies has suggested second string football is the next step for Chang on his road to recovery. Alfie Chang is on track to return to the field before the end of the year after 14 months without pulling on Birmingham City’s colours.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City will be without four players for Northampton Town clash
arts.sum: Blues will still be without wingers Emil Hansson and Scott Wright, while Lee Buchanan is being given time to build up his fitness after more than three months on the sidelines. Buchanan is being put through a mini pre-season having sustained an injury in Blues' penultimate warm up game in July.

arts.ttl: Latest Birmingham City sell-out could lead to chaos after ‘disappointing’ decision
arts.sum: A comment issued to BirminghamLive reads: “Whilst there have been issues with overcrowding at Bordesley Station this season it’s disappointing that West Midlands Rail have sought to address this problem by just removing trains in one direction - this is poor customer service. West Midlands Rail confirmed earlier this week that supporters will no longer be able to travel back to the city centre from Bordesley, which is the closest train station to St Andrew’s.

arts.ttl: Davies could be about to make his most ruthless Birmingham City decision yet
arts.sum: “I want difficult decisions and I want there to be competition for places and everything else that comes with that,” the Blues manager says of his centre-back conundrum. As we know, Blues’ 4-2-3-1 formation becomes a 3-2-5 in possession and Bielik sees a lot of the ball in the middle of the back three.

arts.sum: Birmingham City defender Krystian Bielik is back in contention to play this weekend, as per a report by… The post Update emerges regarding Birmingham City man’s injury ahead of Northampton Town game appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Ashley Cole and two former Birmingham City bosses in running to become new Coventry manager
arts.sum: Two former Birmingham City managers and recently departed assistant boss Ashley Cole are in the running to become Coventry City’s new chief after the sacking of Mark Robins. Gary Rowett is also a 20/1 bet having been out of work since leaving Blues at the end of last season.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City receive huge injury boost before Northampton Town clash
arts.sum: Blues captain Bielik has come through training unscathed this week and should come into contention for a place in the squad to face Northampton Town. In the six games Bielik has missed, loanee Ben Davies has partnered Christoph Klarer at the back to keep Blues ticking along at the top of League One.
